title: Deep .py consolidation (disentangled) - r0_migration tool cluster
registered_by: user directive: 'Tiefe .py-Konsolidierung (entflochten) -> erst nach M1/M2-Seal (live zu riskant)'
principle: ONE driver -> ONE conductor -> phase runners -> ONE guard -> ONE executor, supported by a small core-lib set. Rival autonomous drivers and duplicated friction utilities are retired. RETIRE != blind delete: several 'rival' modules export PURE helpers that the canonical chain imports (e.g. sla_enforce.canonical_r0_dir is used by run_r0_migration_phase_m1). Disentangle = extract the still-needed pure helpers into the core lib FIRST, then remove the driver loop + friction.
hard_invariant: After EVERY step the canonical chain must (a) import cleanly, (b) py_compile cleanly, (c) pass seal_r0_migration_phase.py --verify-only for all already-sealed phases. Any regression -> revert that step (fail-closed).

gating.prefer_host: WSL ~/active_alpha_model (not Windows E: — avoid consolidate-then-rsync waste)
gating.reason_live_risky: The cluster is import-entangled; editing while M1 backtest + autoseal are live could break the seal pipeline mid-flight.

dependency_map_basis: derived from import grep over tools/ (2026-06-05). Canonical reachability = imported (transitively) by _m1_autoseal -> orchestrator / phase runners / seal / phase_guard / validation_matrix / outage_guard.

KEEP_core_lib: tools/r0_migration_commander.py (PURE process-control helpers _migration_pids/_kill_pids/_lock_holder_pid/_pid_alive - keep; RETIRE only its autonomous run_commander loop)
tools/r0_migration_outage_guard.py (CPU-aware stall repair; fold recovery here)
tools/r0_migration_killer_pack.py (subprocess env + kill - used by validation_matrix)

MERGE.why: five overlapping stop/kill/prune utilities -> one process-control surface
duplicated M1 status/progress/scope reporting -> one status surface. Drop dead M1_ENTRY_BAT (.bat launchers already removed).

MERGE.then_retire: the SLA *enforcement* friction (enforce_sla_fast_path / sla_fast_path_active deadline logic) - already neutered via m1_sla_6h.json
MERGE.why: phase_m1/commander/reach_milestone import pure path helpers from sla_enforce; the deadline friction (A-gate) must go without breaking those imports

RETIRE_rival_drivers: tools/r0_migration_finish_push.py (only reached via retired drivers + scheduled_worker; verify scheduled_worker no longer needs it, else fold the seal-attempt into orchestrator)

execution_order: 0. PRECONDITION: M1 sealed AND M2 sealed AND explicit go.
1. Snapshot: git status clean; record canonical verify-only PASS baseline for M0/M1/M2.
2. Extract pure helpers (sla_enforce path helpers, commander process helpers) into core lib; rewire imports. Verify chain.
3. MERGE processctl cluster; rewire callers; verify chain.
4. MERGE status cluster; rewire callers; verify chain.
5. Fold recovery into outage_guard; verify chain.
6. RETIRE rival drivers (delete) once nothing canonical imports them (grep must return only deleted-among-deleted).
7. Final: import+compile+verify-only PASS on M0/M1/M2; one driver (_m1_autoseal) + one conductor (orchestrator) remain as entry points.
validation_each_step: python -c import of the full canonical chain
py_compile of canonical chain + edited modules
import grep: retired module names must have ZERO remaining importers
do_not: do not change any gate logic or thresholds (B-gates intact)
do not alter strategy returns / numerical outputs (this is tooling-only)
do not run during a live M1/M2 backtest
preparation_done_now: read-only dependency map + classification registered. Execute on WSL after M1+M2 seal + explicit go. See migration_efficiency_plan.json.
